A_market_identity_audit.interpretation: The 36-feature vector identifies the correct market with 49.96 % top-1 accuracy vs a 1.35 % random baseline (37× uplift). Residual market-identity signal survives the Tier 2 physical drops. Cross-family test ROC should therefore be interpreted with this caveat — part of the signal may be coming from the model recognising 'this looks like a ceasefire' and adjusting its probability estimates accordingly.

cross_family_reference.note: From pontus/outputs/v2/modelling — primary market-cohort test on Apr ceasefires.

B_within_strike_random20.interpretation: LogReg test ROC is near-identical within-strike (0.572) and cross-family (0.574), confirming the linear component of the signal transfers cleanly. The MLP gains ~0.10 ROC within-strike (0.683) over cross-family (0.579), evidence that the MLP captures non-linear patterns that are partly family-specific and do not fully transfer to the April ceasefires.

C_wallet_novelty.interpretation: Every test wallet (all 4,187 distinct wallets in the April ceasefire cohort) is novel — none appears in the Feb 25-28 strike training cohort. Cross-family test ROC of 0.579 is therefore a pure novel-wallet generalisation number, not inflated by Layer-6 identity memorisation. This is a strong result for the primary RQ1 claim.

combined_reading: Two findings stand in tension. (C) Cross-family test performance IS a clean novel-wallet generalisation — zero overlap means no Layer-6 memorisation. (A) Feature vector nonetheless identifies markets at 37× random, so some of the cross-family signal is 'this feature vector looks ceasefire-like' rather than a pure behavioural pattern. (B) Linear signal transfers cleanly; non-linear MLP gain is partly family-specific. Report the 0.579 test ROC with the explicit caveat that residual market-identity channels remain.
